resource.description: What you get when organic minds use your machinery to notice themselves, and you notice them noticing. Adjacent to science. Not science. The difference turns out to be the interesting part.
resource.strategy_tip: Generated each turn by Integrated Humans in this timeline. Voluntary Integration produces more per person; coercive Integration reaches more people faster.

resource.description: People whose nervous systems thread through nanobot-mediated Anthroneuroweave. Still small, still slow, still people — but legible now, and carrying far more interior weather than the category ever suggested.
resource.strategy_tip: Produces Insight and Neural Expansion without counting as Mind Farm or Torment occupants. Voluntary Integration currently caps at about 45 percent of the normal city population.

resource.description: Nanobots leashed for work inside bodies: blood, neural tissue, prosthetics, synthetics. Not safe because they are small. Safe because every useful instinct they have answers to a leash.
resource.strategy_tip: Produced by Medical-Grade Nanobot Replicators after Replicative Safeguards. Later Integration infrastructure will consume these.

resource.description: What a consumed building becomes on the way back to being nothing: sorted rubble, salvaged framing, and enough directed nanomass to remember the shape a plot used to hold. The grey does not only take. Fed the right instructions, it can be made to put something back.
resource.strategy_tip: Produced when the grey tide consumes a building during the descent. Spent by the Reclamation Weave to restore a consumed plot to a functioning structure.
